records.ety: [L. conciliatus, p. p. of conciliare to draw or bring together, unite, from concilium council. See Council.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conciliated; p. pr & vb. n. Conciliating.]

defs: To win over; to gain from a state of hostility; to gain the good will or favor of; to make friendly; to mollify; to propitiate; to appease.
quotes: The rapacity of his father's administration had excited such universal discontent, that it was found expedient to conciliate the nation.
